assumptions: The dividend receivable is a recognised foreign-currency intragroup monetary item.
The related exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation and could affect consolidated profit or loss.
The entity has an eligible hedging instrument and can satisfy IFRS 9 designation, documentation and effectiveness requirements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised foreign-currency intragroup receivable can be assessed under the fair value hedge model because IFRS 9 permits hedging a recognised asset's risk exposure that could affect profit or loss, subject to the intragroup foreign-currency exception in consolidation.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The same exposure can also be assessed under the cash flow hedge model because IFRS 9 permits hedging variability in cash flows of a recognised asset attributable to a particular risk that could affect profit or loss.
